verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via dual N-terminal lipid modifications: co-translational myristoylation at Gly2 and post-translational palmitoylation at Cys3. All functional domains (SH4, SH3, SH2, kinase) face the cytoplasm; there is no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no extracellular domain. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented outer-leaflet display in cancer, apoptosis (caspase cleavage removes the anchor and releases LYN to cytosol, not to outer leaflet), activation, stress, or ICD; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no outer-leaflet display in any cell lineage; inner-leaflet lipid anchor is the invariant mechanism; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — LYN is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — LYN has a well-documented PM pool but strictly on the cytoplasmic face; inner-leaflet anchoring does not confer outer-leaflet accessibility; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no evidence of wash-resistant covalent or non-covalent tethering to a TM scaffold exposing any LYN domain extracellularly. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic (antibody/ADC/CAR-T) engages the LYN protein body from outside the cell.
